NCT04184622.name: SURMOUNT-1
NCT04184622.group: Adults with obesity without T2D, tirzepatide 15 mg SC weekly vs placebo (wk 72, % weight change MD). Trial design: 4-arm RCT (n≈2,539) randomising tirzepatide 5 mg + 10 mg + 15 mg + placebo. This entry uses the 15 mg arm only (FDA-approved high dose); 5 mg and 10 mg arms are NOT extracted but available in Jastreboff 2022 NEJM Table 2 if dose-response sensitivity is needed.

allOutcomes.title: Percent change in body weight from baseline at week 72 (primary, tirzepatide 15 mg minus placebo, MD)
% body-weight change at week 72, tirzepatide 5 mg vs placebo (MD; SURMOUNT-1)
% body-weight change at week 72, tirzepatide 10 mg vs placebo (MD; SURMOUNT-1)
% body-weight change at week 72, tirzepatide 15 mg vs placebo (MD; SURMOUNT-1)

NCT04184622.snippet: Source: Jastreboff AM et al. NEJM 2022;387:205-216 (SURMOUNT-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 2,539 adults with BMI >=30 (or >=27 with at least one weight-related comorbidity) WITHOUT type 2 diabetes randomized 1:1:1:1 double-blind to tirzepatide 5 mg, 10 mg, 15 mg, or placebo subcutaneous weekly for 72 weeks. This app analyses the 15 mg vs placebo comparison (n=630 vs n=643).

evidence.label: Primary - % Body Weight Change at Week 72
evidence.text: Mean percentage change in body weight from baseline to week 72: tirzepatide 15 mg -20.9% vs placebo -3.1%; estimated treatment difference -17.8 percentage points (95% CI -18.9 to -16.6; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met with unprecedented magnitude for pharmacotherapy in non-T2D obesity.

evidence.label: Secondary - Categorical Weight Loss and CV Metabolic Markers
evidence.text: >=20% weight loss: 57% tirz 15 mg vs 3% placebo. >=15% weight loss: 78% vs 10%. Improvements in HbA1c, fasting insulin, lipids, blood pressure. Discontinuation due to AEs 7% vs 3%. GI AEs (nausea, diarrhoea, constipation) most common; generally mild-moderate.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 79% treatment completion (typical for obesity pharmacotherapy trials). D4 LOW - central labs. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Body mass Index (BMI) ≥30 kilograms per square meter (kg/m²), or ≥27 kg/m² and previous diagnosis with at least one of the following comorbidities: hypertension, dyslipidemia, obstructive sleep apnea, cardiovascular disease~* History of at least one unsuccessful dietary effort to lose body weight~Exclusion Criteria:~* Diabetes mellitus~* Change in body weight greater than 5 kg within 3 months prior to starting study~* Obesity induced by other endocrinologic disorders or mo...

NCT04657003.name: SURMOUNT-2
NCT04657003.group: Adults with obesity AND T2D, tirzepatide 15 mg SC weekly vs placebo (wk 72, % weight change MD)

allOutcomes.title: Percent change in body weight from baseline at week 72 (primary, tirzepatide 15 mg minus placebo, MD)

NCT04657003.snippet: Source: Garvey WT et al. Lancet 2023;402:613-626 (SURMOUNT-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 938 adults with BMI >=27 AND type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 7-10%) randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to tirzepatide 10 mg, tirzepatide 15 mg, or placebo SC weekly for 72 weeks. This app analyses the 15 mg vs placebo comparison (n=312 vs n=315).

evidence.label: Primary - % Body Weight Change at Week 72
evidence.text: Mean percentage change in body weight at week 72: tirzepatide 15 mg -14.7% vs placebo -3.2%; estimated treatment difference -11.5 percentage points (95% CI -12.8 to -10.3; P<0.0001). Primary met; effect smaller than in SURMOUNT-1 non-T2D population.

evidence.label: Secondary - HbA1c, Categorical Weight Loss
evidence.text: >=15% weight loss: 57% tirz 15 mg vs 3% placebo. HbA1c change: -2.1% tirz vs -0.5% placebo. Most patients below HbA1c 5.7%. GI AEs (nausea, diarrhoea) typical class effect. Severe hypoglycaemia rare.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by baseline HbA1c and BMI. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 85% completion. D4 LOW - central labs. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have Type 2 Diabetes (T2DM) with HbA1c ≥7% to ≤10% at screening, on stable therapy for the last 3 months prior to screening. T2DM may be treated with diet/exercise alone or any oral glycemic-lowering agent (as per local labeling) EXCEPT dipeptidyl peptidase 4 (DPP-4) inhibitors or glucagon like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1 RAs)~* Have a BMI of ≥27 kg/m²~* Are overweight or have obesity~* Have a history of at least 1 self-reported unsuccessful dietary effort to lose b...

NCT04657016.name: SURMOUNT-3
NCT04657016.group: Adults with obesity after 12-week intensive-lifestyle lead-in, tirzepatide max-tolerated vs placebo (wk 0-72, % weight change from lead-in end, MD)

allOutcomes.title: Percent change in body weight from randomisation to week 72 (primary, tirzepatide minus placebo, MD)

NCT04657016.snippet: Source: Wadden TA et al. Nat Med 2023;29:2909-2918 (SURMOUNT-3).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 579 adults with BMI >=30 (or >=27 + comorbidity) who lost >=5% body weight during a 12-week open-label intensive-lifestyle lead-in phase randomized 1:1 double-blind to tirzepatide max-tolerated dose (10/15 mg) or matched placebo SC weekly for 72 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - % Body Weight Change Randomisation to Week 72
evidence.text: Mean percentage change in body weight from randomisation to week 72: tirzepatide -18.4% vs placebo +2.5% (regain); estimated treatment difference -20.9 percentage points (95% CI -22.7 to -19.0; P<0.0001). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Categorical Weight Loss Maintenance
evidence.text: >=5% additional weight loss: 88% tirz vs 17% placebo. >=15% additional weight loss from randomisation: 48% vs 2%. Total cumulative weight loss from start of lead-in: approximately -26% tirz vs -3% placebo.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 86% completion. D4 LOW - central labs. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have a body mass index (BMI) ≥30 kg/m2 or ≥27 kg/m2 and previously diagnosed with at least 1 of the following comorbidities: hypertension, dyslipidemia, obstructive sleep apnea or cardiovascular disease~* History of at least one unsuccessful dietary effort to lose body weight~Exclusion Criteria:~* Diabetes mellitus~* Change in body weight greater than 5 kg within 3 months prior to starting study~* Obesity induced by other endocrinologic disorders or monogenetic or syndromi...

title: RapidMeta Endocrinology | Tirzepatide for Weight Management (SURMOUNT) v1.1
